sections.paragraph:
φωτίζω [φῶς] ‘cause to be bright with light’

—a. provide illumination, w. focus on benefit provided by light Rv 18:1; 21:23; φωτίσει ἐπ’ αὐτούς provide light for them 22:5 (without ἐπί v.l.; cp. Lk 11:36, where light expedites ease of movement). This function appears in a transf. sense

—b. of transcendent illumination that reveals or exposes bring to light: personal behavior 1 Cor 4:5; God’s secret plan Eph 3:9; what is in store for believers in the gospel 2 Ti 1:10. Pass., of being the beneficiary of divine illumination for appropriate response to God, be enlightened Eph 1:18; Hb 6:4; 10:32.—J 1:9 sums the idea: τὸ φῶς τὸ ἀληϑινὸν φωτίζει πάντα ἄνϑρωπον the true light provides illumination for every person.
